page: Microsoft Azure F8 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the F family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 131 GB, and there are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile characterized by average multi-core capabilities alongside weak single-core execution. The server achieves an average rating in multi-core metrics, such as a stress-ng multi-core score of 6598.92 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4494.0. However, single-core performance is poor, falling into the bottom 10% with a Geekbench single-core score of 812.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 955.58. Memory performance is mixed; read bandwidth is average at 52.57 GB/sec for large blocks, but write bandwidth is weak at 23.47 GB/sec.

The F8 instance presents a distinct tradeoff between compute density and single-threaded speed. While its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio aligns with compute-optimized profiles, the older Intel Xeon architecture limits its performance in modern single-threaded tasks like text processing and compression. Qualitatively, it offers a cost-efficient option for parallelized workloads that do not require high memory-per-core ratios or the latest instruction sets. Suitable workloads include static web serving, Redis database operations, software compilation, and basic multi-threaded processing.
description: Microsoft Azure F8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the F family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 8 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.3 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 131 GB of bundled local storage. The instance does not include a GPU. Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between average multi-core capabilities and poor single-core execution. Multi-core metrics, such as a Geekbench multi-core score of 4494.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 6598.92, fall in the average tier, whereas single-core benchmarks reside in the bottom 10%. Memory write bandwidth is weak at 23.47 GB/sec, though read bandwidth remains average. This hardware configuration is suited for parallelized workloads like static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation, offering a balanced compute-to-RAM ratio for non-memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB of RAM, and 131 GB of local storage, delivering average multi-core and poor single-core performance.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring eight dedicated cores, bundled local storage, and balanced multi-core performance for parallelized workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Bundled 131 GB of local storage with no GPU accelerators
Average multi-core performance contrasted by poor single-core benchmark results
Best suited for static web serving, Redis operations, and software compilation
